summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary free‑park show highlighted by a smoking Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s sequence and fiery Truckin’ jams.
review: The fink‑9392 SBD is praised for its crisp, well‑balanced mix that lets the band’s 1975 magic shine.  The performance is lauded for its immediate energy – no warm‑up songs, a soaring Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s run, a blistering Truckin’ jam and strong TLEO – while a handful of reviewers note a weak Help intro or sloppy lyrics on Truckin’.  Overall the show is regarded as one of the era’s gems.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80% of reviews are 4‑5 stars and repeatedly praise both the performance and the soundboard quality; only a few dissenting comments keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clear and well‑balanced; minor notes of slight softness or occasional drop‑outs but no serious flaws.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
King Solomon's Marbles / Stronger Than Dirt
poor_songs: Help on the Way (weak lead‑in noted by a single reviewer)
Truckin' (lyrics slippage mentioned by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set jumps straight into a tight, lyrical opening and stays high‑energy throughout; fans note the lack of typical warm‑up numbers gives the show an immediate, “lucid” feel that builds to powerful jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring lead guitar tone, especially on Franklin's Tower and the Help>Slipknot sequence; a few critics felt his Help intro was weak.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted as funky, high in the mix and “incredible” by several listeners.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as tight; occasional mention of solid rhythm guitar work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's Fender Rhodes praised for tasty comping; Matthew Kelly’s harmonica noted on a few tracks.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey’s dual‑drum texture especially praised during the Truckin’ jam.
